title: Listening - Part 3
description: For each question, write the correct answer in the gap. Write one or two words or a number or a date or a time.
instructions: You will hear one person giving information. For questions 14–19, write the correct answer in the gap.
context: You will hear a woman giving information to visitors at a college open day.
audio_script: Woman: Hello, and welcome to our college open day. I'm going to give you a quick guide to the morning, then you can visit the departments that interest you. First, everyone should collect a map from the table near the [14]stairs[/14]. The college has three buildings, and visitors sometimes get lost. The first talk is about choosing the right course. It begins at [15]ten thirty[/15] in the main hall. After the talk, teachers will be available in their classrooms. If you are interested in science, please go to Room [16]twelve[/16], where students are showing some of their recent projects. The sports department is not in the main building. You need to cross the car park and go through the blue [17]gate[/17]. There will be signs to help you. Free drinks are available in the café, but lunch is not free. A sandwich and fruit will cost [18]three[/18] pounds today. If you apply for a course before you leave, staff at the admissions desk can check your form. You will find the admissions desk beside the [19]bookshop[/19]. Please ask any student in a red T-shirt if you need help. Parents and friends are welcome to come with you today, but the talks are mainly for students. Please take any leaflets that interest you and write down questions as you go round. Staff will be happy to speak to you after each short presentation. Please check the notice board before you leave, because small changes to rooms or times may be written there. If you are not sure about anything, ask a member of staff.

questions.explanation: The speaker says visitors should collect a map near the stairs.
The speaker says the first talk begins at ten thirty.
The speaker says science projects are in Room twelve.
The speaker says people should go through the blue gate.
The speaker says a sandwich and fruit will cost three pounds.
The speaker says the admissions desk is beside the bookshop.
